about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Metz Jr.
about_quotes.evidence: Butch Miles, who was my teacher when I was growing up for a year. He was my first drum teacher.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Metz Jr. interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, October 3, 2015

about_quotes.speaker: Peter Appleyard
about_quotes.evidence: The record I made over in Barbados was done with Bucky, Major Holly, Butch Miles, Rick Wilkins on tenor, and myself
about_quotes.doc_title: Peter Appleyard interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, October 4, 2012

about_quotes.speaker: Count Basie
about_quotes.evidence: I'm very happy with Butch... he's the guy I was looking for... Why did I hire him?

about_quotes.speaker: Duffy Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: Butch Miles, bless his heart, I thought he was never going to leave the band because he was there almost five years... Butch wanted to give a whole bunch of different drummers an opportunity to play with the band... Count Basie and Butch Miles gave me seven months to prepare. I had seven months know
about_quotes.doc_title: Duffy Jackson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 17, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Bley Carla
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Butch Miles on Drums... [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah it just was great no matter where he was. He could play the Hollywood Bowl and sound great.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bley Carla — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Segal
about_quotes.evidence: Duffy, Wayne and I went down to Florida and did some stuff around Tampa, and we had the band... Butch Miles was with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Segal — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
